title: urvillea uniloba f. genuina
intro: Urvillea uniloba f. genuina is a plant species, also known as the "False Copal", that belongs to the Rutaceae family. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating various health issues. The plant's medicinal properties are attributed to its flavonoid and terpenoid compounds, which have been found to exhibit health benefits such as wound healing, pain relief, and antimicrobial activity. Urvillea uniloba f. genuina can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to alleviate symptoms of colds, fever, and digestive issues.
